description: Fix a validation inconsistency in the dev-mode file watcher: parseToolsJson in file-watcher.ts silently defaults missing required tool fields (input_schema) to empty objects, allowing tools with effectively no input validation to be registered when tools.json changes on disk. The initial plugin load path (validateTools in loader.ts) correctly rejects such malformed manifests. Align the file watcher path to use the same strict validation.

userStories.title: Replace lenient parseToolsJson with strict validateTools in file watcher
userStories.description: handleToolsJsonChange in file-watcher.ts uses parseToolsJson which defaults missing input_schema to {} and missing output_schema to {}, allowing malformed tools to be registered when tools.json is updated in dev mode. Replace the call to parseToolsJson with extractToolsArray + validateTools (already used by loader.ts) so that a tools.json with missing required fields causes a log error and no registry update, matching the behavior of the initial plugin load.
acceptanceCriteria: handleToolsJsonChange calls validateTools (imported from loader.ts) instead of parseToolsJson after extracting the tools array
When validateTools returns an error result, handleToolsJsonChange logs the error and returns without updating the registry
When validateTools returns a success result, the registry is updated normally as before
parseToolsJson is either removed or no longer called from handleToolsJsonChange
handleIifeChange and handlePendingPluginChange are NOT changed — only handleToolsJsonChange
